hero.headline: MAHE Online MBA in Logistics & Supply Chain Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: NAAC A++ | NIRF #3 University 2025 | Institution of Eminence | UGC-DEB Approved | Total fees ₹2,92,000

sections.tldr: MAHE Online MBA in Logistics & Supply Chain Management covers supply chain management, logistics fundamentals, operations management, quality management, procurement, and logistics distribution. NAAC A++ NIRF #3 University with IoE tag. Total fee ₹2,92,000 (₹73,000/sem). Zero-cost EMI ₹12,167/month. Coursera Plus bundled free. 8 scholarship categories. Work experience required; fresh graduates submit SOP video.

about.body:
MAHE's Online MBA in Logistics & Supply Chain Management covers end-to-end supply chain planning, procurement, and distribution. Semester 3 covers supply chain management (demand planning, inventory optimisation, supplier relationship management, bullwhip effect mitigation), fundamentals of logistics management (warehousing, transportation modes, 3PL/4PL models, freight management), and operations management (capacity planning, production scheduling, lean manufacturing basics). Semester 4 adds quality management in logistics and supply chain (ISO standards for logistics, Six Sigma in warehousing, KPI dashboards for supply chain performance), procurement and purchasing (strategic sourcing, vendor evaluation, contract negotiation, e-procurement), and logistics and distribution management (last-mile delivery optimisation, route planning, cold chain logistics, reverse logistics).

The dedicated logistics focus sets this apart from generic operations MBAs. MAHE offers both supply chain strategy subjects and logistics execution subjects, covering the full spectrum from demand planning through last-mile delivery.

MAHE holds NIRF #3 University 2025, NAAC A++, and Institution of Eminence status. Coursera Plus is bundled free. At ₹2,92,000, the fee is premium. The NIRF #3 credential carries weight with large logistics firms and FMCG companies that filter on university rank.

Work experience in reputed organisations is required. Fresh graduates can apply with an SOP video.

EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Semesters 1-2 cover the common MBA management core. Semesters 3-4 below show the Logistics & Supply Chain Management specialisation subjects.

subjects.name: Management Accounting
subjects.desc: Cost analysis, budgeting, variance analysis, and management decision-making using accounting data.

subjects.name: Legal Aspects of Business
subjects.desc: Contract law, consumer protection, intellectual property, company law basics, and regulatory compliance.

subjects.name: Minor Project
subjects.desc: Applied supply chain or logistics research project supervised by faculty.

subjects.name: Supply Chain Management
subjects.desc: Demand planning and forecasting, inventory optimisation (EOQ, safety stock), supplier relationship management, bullwhip effect analysis, and supply chain network design.

subjects.name: Fundamentals of Logistics Management
subjects.desc: Warehousing design and management, transportation mode selection, 3PL and 4PL models, freight management, customs and trade compliance basics.

subjects.name: Operations Management
subjects.desc: Capacity planning, production scheduling, lean manufacturing basics, facility layout design, and aggregate production planning.

subjects.name: Entrepreneurship and Innovation
subjects.desc: Startup ideation, business model canvas, lean startup methodology, and funding strategies.

subjects.name: Sustainability
subjects.desc: Sustainable business models, ESG frameworks, green supply chain, and CSR strategy.

subjects.name: Capstone Project
subjects.desc: Supply chain capstone integrating Sem 1-4 learning with an industry-linked logistics or procurement problem.

subjects.name: Quality Management in Logistics & Supply Chain
subjects.desc: ISO standards for logistics operations, Six Sigma application in warehousing, KPI dashboards for supply chain performance, and vendor quality auditing.

subjects.name: Procurement and Purchasing
subjects.desc: Strategic sourcing, vendor evaluation and selection, contract negotiation, e-procurement platforms, total cost of ownership analysis, and make-vs-buy decisions.

subjects.name: Logistics and Distribution Management
subjects.desc: Last-mile delivery optimisation, route planning algorithms, cold chain logistics, reverse logistics, distribution centre design, and omni-channel distribution strategy.

whoHires.body:
MAHE MBA Logistics & SCM graduates target roles across supply chain, logistics, procurement, and consulting:

**Industries:** E-commerce logistics, FMCG distribution, manufacturing supply chain, third-party logistics (3PL), pharmaceutical logistics, cold chain, automotive, and retail

**Entry-level roles:** Supply Chain Analyst, Logistics Coordinator, Procurement Executive, Warehouse Operations Associate, Demand Planner, Transportation Analyst

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Supply Chain Manager, Logistics Manager, Procurement Manager, Warehouse Manager, Demand Planning Manager, Distribution Head

**Senior roles (7+ years):** VP Supply Chain, Chief Procurement Officer, Head of Logistics, Director of Distribution, Supply Chain Director, COO

**Employers:** E-commerce (Amazon India, Flipkart), 3PL (Delhivery, Blue Dart, DHL, FedEx), FMCG (HUL, ITC, Nestle), manufacturing (Tata Motors, Maruti, L&T), and consulting (McKinsey Operations, Deloitte SCM, Kearney).

**Note:** MAHE provides career support services. India's logistics sector is growing rapidly with GatiShakti and the National Logistics Policy driving demand for qualified SCM professionals.

skills.body:
The Logistics & SCM specialisation builds skills across supply chain strategy, logistics execution, and procurement:

**Technical skills:**
- Supply Chain Planning: demand forecasting methods, EOQ/safety stock calculation, supply chain network optimisation, and bullwhip effect mitigation
- Logistics: warehouse layout design, transportation mode selection, route optimisation, 3PL/4PL evaluation, and cold chain management
- Procurement: strategic sourcing methodology, vendor scorecard design, TCO analysis, and contract drafting
- Quality: ISO audit preparation for logistics, Six Sigma application in warehouse operations, and supply chain KPI dashboard design

**Applied skills:**
- Distribution: last-mile delivery strategy, reverse logistics programme design, omni-channel fulfilment planning
- Operations: capacity planning, production scheduling, and lean waste elimination

**Soft skills:** Negotiating with suppliers, managing cross-functional SCM teams, presenting supply chain cost-reduction proposals to leadership, and coordinating multi-stakeholder logistics operations.

comparisons.body:
Comparing MAHE Online MBA Logistics & SCM against peer programmes:

**vs MUJ Online MBA Supply Chain Management:** MUJ at approximately ₹1,80,000 on the same platform. MAHE at ₹2,92,000 costs ₹1,12,000 more for the NIRF #3 and IoE brand. Both share similar LMS delivery. MAHE offers both supply chain strategy and logistics execution subjects. The content overlap is significant. Unless the MAHE flagship brand specifically matters for your employer, MUJ is strong value.

**vs Symbiosis Online MBA Logistics & SCM:** Symbiosis at approximately ₹2,05,000 with NAAC A+ and strong Pune industry connections. MAHE at ₹2,92,000 costs ₹87,000 more but carries NIRF #3 and NAAC A++ (vs A+). Symbiosis has a dedicated logistics alumni network in Western India.

**vs CU Online MBA Logistics & SCM:** CU at ₹1,65,000 with NIRF #19 and PwC co-teaching. MAHE at ₹2,92,000 costs ₹1,27,000 more. CU's PwC partnership adds industry credibility. MAHE wins on NIRF rank, but CU offers much stronger value for money.

**vs LPU Online MBA Logistics & SCM:** LPU at approximately ₹1,60,000 with NIRF #19 and NAAC A++. Less than half the MAHE price. LPU has active placement support. MAHE wins on NIRF rank and IoE tag.

fees.body:
The MAHE Online MBA in Logistics & Supply Chain Management has a total programme fee of ₹2,92,000 inclusive of all taxes.

**Semester-wise fee breakdown:**
- Semester 1: ₹73,000
- Semester 2: ₹73,000
- Semester 3: ₹73,000
- Semester 4: ₹73,000
- **Total: ₹2,92,000**

**Application fee:** ₹500 one-time.

**Zero-cost EMI:** ₹12,167/month directly through the university.

**Scholarships (8 categories):**
- Defence Personnel / Ex-servicemen: 20%
- Divyaang / PwD: 20%
- Merit: 10% (75%+ aggregate)
- MAHE/AGE Alumni: 15%
- SAARC and EWC Nationals: 40%
- Female Learners: 15%
- Doctors: 15%
- Corporate Employees: 15%

**Important:** Learners opting for loan or financing are not eligible for additional discounts or offers.

**Bundled:** Coursera Plus access free with the programme.

Fees listed are indicative. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with MAHE each quarter. Please reconfirm with our counsellor before any payment.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive an MBA degree certificate with Logistics & Supply Chain Management as the specialisation, issued by Manipal Academy of Higher Education (MAHE) under UGC-DEB 2020 online mode standards.

The degree carries NAAC A++ accreditation, NIRF #3 University rank, Institution of Eminence status, and WES recognition. Valid for supply chain leadership promotions, PSU logistics roles, MNC procurement positions, and private-sector operations recruitment. DigiLocker issuance is standard.

reviews.intro: Three reviews from MAHE Online MBA Logistics & SCM students. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Karthik V.
items.liked: Logistics and Distribution Management covered last-mile and cold chain in practical depth. Procurement module was well-structured with TCO analysis
items.disliked: ₹2,92,000 is hard to justify when MUJ offers supply chain management on the same platform for ₹1,80,000. The LMS experience is identical
items.body: I chose MAHE for the NIRF #3 credential because large logistics firms (DHL, Delhivery) filter on university rank for manager-level hiring. The Logistics and Distribution Management subject covering last-mile, cold chain, and reverse logistics was immediately applicable to my e-commerce operations role. The honest comparison is that MUJ offers nearly identical content for ₹1,12,000 less. The MAHE premium is purely brand value.

items.name: Priyanka D.
items.liked: Quality Management in Logistics was a unique subject. Coursera Plus gave access to supply chain courses from Rutgers and Georgia Tech
items.disliked: Some logistics case studies were India-centric. Limited coverage of international trade compliance and Incoterms
items.body: Working in pharmaceutical distribution, the cold chain logistics and quality management subjects were directly relevant. MAHE's combination of supply chain strategy and logistics execution in one programme was the deciding factor over generic operations MBAs. Coursera Plus supplemented well with Georgia Tech's Supply Chain Fundamentals. NIRF #3 and IoE cleared credential filters at pharma MNCs. The 15% corporate scholarship brought costs to around ₹2,48,200.

items.name: Deepak L.
items.liked: Supply Chain Management fundamentals were solid. Operations Management provided good capacity planning coverage
items.disliked: Career placement support was minimal. The loan-scholarship restriction caught me off guard after admission
items.body: The SCM curriculum covers the right topics. My frustration was the scholarship situation. I qualified for the defence personnel 20% scholarship but also needed a loan. MAHE's policy disqualifies scholarships when you take financing. I ended up paying full ₹2,92,000. CU at ₹1,65,000 with PwC co-teaching would have been better value. If you can pay upfront and qualify for a scholarship, MAHE makes sense. If you need a loan, consider cheaper alternatives.

faqs.question: Does the programme cover both supply chain strategy and logistics execution?
faqs.answer: Yes. Sem 3 covers supply chain strategy (demand planning, inventory optimisation, supplier management) and logistics fundamentals (warehousing, transportation, 3PL). Sem 4 adds procurement, quality management, and distribution management covering last-mile and cold chain.

faqs.question: How does MAHE Logistics & SCM compare to MUJ SCM?
faqs.answer: Same platform, similar delivery. MAHE at ₹2,92,000 carries NIRF #3 and IoE. MUJ at ₹1,80,000 costs ₹1,12,000 less. Choose MAHE if the flagship brand specifically matters for large logistics employers. Choose MUJ if fee value is the priority.

faqs.question: Is cold chain logistics covered?
faqs.answer: Yes. The Logistics and Distribution Management subject in Sem 4 covers cold chain logistics, last-mile delivery optimisation, reverse logistics, and distribution centre design.

faqs.question: Can loan applicants get scholarships?
faqs.answer: No. Learners opting for loan or financing are not eligible for additional discounts or offers. This is a critical consideration when comparing effective costs across programmes.

faqs.question: What is the total fee?
faqs.answer: ₹2,92,000 total (₹73,000 per semester). ₹500 application fee. Zero-cost EMI at ₹12,167/month. 8 scholarship categories available (up to 40%). Reconfirm with our counsellor before payment.

chooseIf: NIRF #3 and Institution of Eminence credential matters for large logistics or FMCG employers.
You want both supply chain strategy and logistics execution depth in one programme.
Coursera Plus access for Georgia Tech and Rutgers supply chain courses adds value.
You qualify for a scholarship category that reduces the ₹2,92,000 effective fee.
consider: MUJ at ₹1,80,000 offers similar platform delivery for ₹1,12,000 less.
CU at ₹1,65,000 includes PwC co-teaching for supply chain content.
Loan financing disqualifies all scholarships, making MAHE one of the most expensive online MBA options.
